name: Activant Solutions (Epicor) Distribution API
what_it_does: Activant Eclipse (now Epicor Eclipse) REST and XML API for specialty wholesale distribution ERP. Enables AI agents to manage sales order and counter sales for branch distribution operations, handle inventory management across multi-branch and warehouse locations, access product catalog and pricing management for specialty distributor sales, retrieve customer account management and credit data for branch sales operations, manage purchasing and vendor replenishment for procurement automation, handle delivery and route management for distribution logistics, access financial management and branch accounting for operational finance, retrieve commission and rebate management for channel compensation, manage EDI and trading partner transaction processing, and integrate Eclipse distribution data with manufacturers, CRM, and e-commerce platforms.
use_cases: Processing counter sales from branch distribution agents
Managing inventory replenishment from supply chain agents
Automating EDI orders from trading partner integration agents
Tracking delivery routes from logistics management agents
not_for: Manufacturing without specialty distribution workflow requirements
General wholesale distribution without electrical/plumbing/HVAC specialty focus
Retail POS without branch distribution counter sales focus
best_when: An electrical, plumbing, HVAC, or industrial specialty wholesale distributor using Activant Eclipse (Epicor Eclipse) wants AI agents to automate branch sales, inventory management, vendor replenishment, and logistics.
avoid_when: INTEGRATION RISK: Activant Eclipse was acquired by Epicor (2011) and has been partially merged into the Epicor platform — API surface reflects legacy Activant architecture mixed with Epicor enhancements. Eclipse XML API is the primary integration path; REST coverage is limited. Branch counter sales automation must handle real-time inventory availability across branches.

auth.notes: Activant Eclipse (now Epicor Eclipse) uses API key and basic authentication. No OAuth 2.0. Eclipse XML API is primary. Austin, Texas HQ (Epicor). Activant Solutions acquired by Epicor (2011). Eclipse serves specialty wholesale distribution — electrical, plumbing, HVAC, industrial. 3,000+ specialty distributor customers. Strong in electrical and mechanical contractor supply. Competes with Infor Distribution, NetSuite, and Epicor P21 for specialty wholesale distribution ERP.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual subscription plus implementation
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription per user and branch
pricing.notes: Austin, Texas (Epicor). Activant founded 1972. Acquired by Epicor (2011). Eclipse product serves electrical, plumbing, HVAC specialty distribution. 3,000+ branch distributor customers. Competes with Infor Distribution SX.e and Epicor P21 for wholesale distribution ERP.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits not publicly documented; server-dependent

known_agent_gotchas: INTEGRATION RISK: Eclipse XML API is legacy architecture; REST coverage is limited; many Eclipse distribution functions require XML API with verbose SOAP-like request structure
Acquisition complexity — Activant Eclipse acquired by Epicor 2011; documentation may reference both Activant and Epicor branding; developer portal at Epicor
No webhooks — Eclipse lacks native event webhooks; event-driven automation requires polling for order status, inventory, and delivery changes
Branch inventory context — Eclipse multi-branch distributors require branch context in all inventory and order queries; wrong branch context returns incorrect stock availability
No OAuth — API key and basic auth only; credential rotation and security hardening requires manual key management
agent_readiness.security_notes: Specialty distribution ERP. SOC2. API key/basic auth. US. Sales order and inventory data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Distribution ERP. SOC2. API key/basic. US. Sales and inventory data.
